content:
Member-only story

I didn’t plan on learning Next.js. A client simply wanted a faster website with better SEO. I figured I’d complete the project, get paid, and move on. Instead, that one project completely changed the type of work I attracted and suddenly I was building AI dashboards, SaaS products, and internal business tools instead of simple websites.

5 min read
7 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Pankaj Patel on Unsplash1. I Thought I Was Building Websites I Was Actually Building Businesses
When I first started freelancing with JavaScript, every project looked familiar.
Landing pages.
Portfolio websites.
Small company sites.
Nothing particularly exciting.
Every client asked for roughly the same things.
A contact form.
A pricing page.
A blog.
Responsive design.
